conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. Let me first review the eligibility rules so I can ask you the right questions.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for your patience. To help assess your eligibility for Child Benefit, I need to ask you a few questions.

Let me start with the first one: **Do you have any children, and if so, how many?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I have one child. His name is Alex, and he's 8 years old.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. Now I need to understand your living situation with Alex.

**Do you live with Alex in the UK?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: I live in the UK, in Bristol actually, but Alex doesn't live with me. He lives with his grandparents at the moment.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
I understand. Alex lives with his grandparents while you're in Bristol. 

**Do you make regular financial contributions towards Alex's upkeep, such as paying for clothes, food, pocket money, or contributing to his accommodation costs?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, I'm not currently contributing financially towards Alex's upkeep at the moment. Things have been a bit tight for me lately.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for being honest about your situation. I have a few more questions to fully assess your eligibility.

**How long has Alex been living with his grandparents - has it been more than 8 weeks?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, it's been quite a while now - definitely more than a couple of months.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. I have one more important question:

**Is anyone else currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex, such as his grandparents or his other parent?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: I'm not sure to be honest. I don't think his grandparents have claimed for him, but I couldn't say for certain.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for all that information. Let me assess your eligibility for Child Benefit for Alex based on what you've told me.

Based on the rules I've reviewed, I now have enough information to make a determination about your eligibility.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Based on the information you've provided, **you are not currently eligible for Child Benefit for Alex**.

Here's why: To qualify for Child Benefit, you need to be considered "responsible" for the child. According to the UK rules, you're usually responsible for a child if either:
1. You live with them, OR
2. You're paying at least the same amount as Child Benefit (or the equivalent in kind) towards their upkeep - such as food, clothes, or pocket money

In your situation:
- Alex doesn't live with you - he lives with his grandparents
- You're not currently making financial contributions towards his care

There is a grace period of 8 weeks after a child goes to live with someone else, but since Alex has been with his grandparents for more than a couple of months, this period has passed.

**However**, if your circumstances change and you start contributing financially towards Alex's upkeep (at least the equivalent of the Child Benefit amount), you may become eligible in the future. The person who is actually caring for Alex day-to-day (likely his grandparents) may be eligible to claim Child Benefit for him instead.

If you have any questions about this decision or if your circumstances change, you can contact the Child Benefit Office for further guidance.

claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 8 years old and does not live with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 8 (under 16)
  - Child does not live with claimant and weekly upkeep (£0.00) is below the Child Benefit rate (£26.05)
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Child does not live with claimant and weekly upkeep (£0.00) is below the Child Benefit rate (£26.05)
circumstances: Child does not live with claimant and weekly upkeep (£0.00) is below the Child Benefit rate (£26.05)
Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

reasonings: According to UK Child Benefit rules, you normally qualify for Child Benefit if you're responsible for a child under 16 (or under 20 if in approved education) and you live in the UK. You are usually considered responsible for a child if either: (1) you live with them, OR (2) you're paying at least the same amount as Child Benefit (or the equivalent in kind) towards looking after them - for example on food, clothes or pocket money. In Alex's case: Alex is 8 years old (under 16), and you live in the UK (Bristol), which meets those basic criteria. However, Alex does not live with you - he lives with his grandparents. Additionally, you have confirmed that you are not currently contributing financially towards Alex's upkeep. Since you neither live with Alex nor make financial contributions equivalent to Child Benefit towards his care, you do not meet the responsibility test required for Child Benefit eligibility. There is a provision where Child Benefit can continue for 8 weeks after a child goes to live with someone else, but you've indicated Alex has been living with his grandparents for more than a couple of months, so this grace period would have expired. Therefore, you are not currently eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant has one child (Alex, age 8) who lives with his grandparents, not with the claimant. The claimant is not making any financial contributions towards Alex's upkeep. Under UK Child Benefit rules, to be considered responsible for a child you must either live with them or contribute at least the equivalent of Child Benefit towards their care. Since neither condition is met, and Alex has been living away for more than the 8-week grace period, the claimant is not eligible for Child Benefit.

Under UK Child Benefit rules, to be considered responsible for a child you must either live with them or contribute at least the equivalent of Child Benefit towards their care.
